conceptual_groups.summary: Ultimatum-branch evidence is still not fully canonical: (a) self_check_issue_count is read from different sources in run-postmortem vs cycle-postmortem when the ultimatum revision runs, and (b) the ultimatum revision's updated compliance map and fix-items map are never persisted to the canonical round-artifact locations before returning.

findings.description: board.ts writeRunPostmortem (line ~794) populates PM-003 self_check_issue_count from latestRound.selfCheckIssues — the pre-ultimatum value stored when executeRound returned. After the ultimatum revision fires (lines 560-587), currentSelfCheckIssues is updated (line 578) but roundResult.selfCheckIssues is not. index.ts writeCyclePostmortem receives boardResult.finalSelfCheckIssues which is the post-ultimatum currentSelfCheckIssues (returned at line 651). These two canonical artifacts will therefore report different self_check_issue_count values for the same run whenever the ultimatum path executes.
findings.source_section: board.ts:writeRunPostmortem (lines 793-797), index.ts:writeCyclePostmortem (lines 477-480), index.ts:executeBoard return at line 651

findings.description: The ultimatum revision (lines 560-587) produces updated complianceMap (line 576) and fixItemsMap (line 577) and writes them to the component-repair-engine bundleDir (final-ultimatum-rN), but never writes them to the canonical round directory (rounds/round-N/compliance-map.json and rounds/round-N/fix-items-map.json). The run-postmortem audit trail (latestArtifactRefs, line 767) references the pre-ultimatum round-level artifacts. The ultimatum revision's evidence is therefore buried in the runtime bundle dir and unreachable via the standard artifact reference chain.
findings.source_section: board.ts:executeBoard lines 259-277 (round-dir artifact writes at round start), lines 560-587 (ultimatum revision), lines 765-768 (latestArtifactRefs pointing to round dir)

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: After the ultimatum revision completes (lines 575-578), write the updated complianceMap and fixItemsMap to the canonical round directory (same paths used at round start: join(roundDir, 'compliance-map.json') and join(roundDir, 'fix-items-map.json')) so the postmortem audit trail references the post-ultimatum state. Additionally, update roundResult.selfCheckIssues (or pass currentSelfCheckIssues explicitly to writeRunPostmortem) so both run-postmortem and cycle-postmortem read self_check_issue_count from the same post-ultimatum source. This is the residual implementation work from prior group-1 redesign guidance: 'persist the matching compliance map, fix-items map... from that object before returning' and 'feed both postmortems from the same explicit arrays.'

conceptual_groups.summary: index.ts countBoardReviewIssues counts all conceptual_groups including any synthetic parse-error group, while board.ts countRoundSeverities only counts groups whose severity matches a known enum value (blocking/major/minor/suggestion), silently dropping others via default:break. Under the current code parse-error groups cannot reach reviewerVerdicts (parse errors throw BoardBlockedError before storing any verdict), so these functions agree in practice. However the divergence is latent: any future path that allows a partial or synthetic verdict into reviewerVerdicts would cause cycle-postmortem review_issue_count > run-postmortem review_issue_count for the same run.

findings.description: index.ts countBoardReviewIssues (lines 658-666) counts verdict.conceptual_groups.length unconditionally. board.ts countRoundSeverities (lines 1183-1208) iterates group.severity and skips unrecognized values (default: break). If a conceptual_group ever has a severity outside {blocking, major, minor, suggestion}, the two PM-003 review_issue_count values will diverge between run-postmortem and cycle-postmortem.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Normalize review_issue_count by using the same counting function in both postmortem writers, or by passing a pre-computed review_issue_count from board.ts (where it is already calculated from severity_counts) into the BoardResult return value so index.ts does not need its own counting logic. Eliminating the duplicate count reduces the coupling surface and ensures both documents are always consistent.

fix_decisions.reason: The implementer's fix unified PM-003 field shape across board-path and zero-round writers, which is correct as far as it goes. But the prior group-1 redesign guidance also required (a) feeding both postmortems from the same explicit arrays and (b) persisting compliance map, fix-items map, and evidence from the ultimatum branch before returning. Neither of these requirements was met: self_check_issue_count in run-postmortem still reads from latestRound.selfCheckIssues (pre-ultimatum) while cycle-postmortem reads from finalSelfCheckIssues (post-ultimatum), and the ultimatum revision's compliance/fix-items output is never written to the canonical round directory.

component-repair-engine/engine.ts now throws on missing fix_items_map section (line 61) and on empty fix_items_map (line 68) in revision mode. Both failure modes are caught before any workflow state can advance. The fail-closed requirement is met.

The PM-003 four-field shape is now uniform across writeRunPostmortem (board.ts) and writeZeroRoundRunPostmortem (index.ts), and validation_issue_count in board.ts now reads from ctx.validationIssues. However the same COD-009 violation persists in the ultimatum branch: writeRunPostmortem's self_check_issue_count comes from latestRound.selfCheckIssues (pre-ultimatum) while writeCyclePostmortem's comes from boardResult.finalSelfCheckIssues (post-ultimatum). The canonical four-field shape requirement was met; the canonical-source requirement was not met for this branch.

writeCyclePostmortem PM-003 now includes review_issue_count: params.reviewIssueCount (line 479) and total_issue_count now sums all three phase counts (line 480). The four-field canonical shape is present and total_issue_count is complete.

residual_risks: Ultimatum path creates a post-revision self_check_issue_count discrepancy between run-postmortem and cycle-postmortem — two canonical audit artifacts for the same run will disagree on this KPI field whenever the ultimatum executes.

Ultimatum revision's compliance map and fix-items map are accessible only via the component-repair-engine bundle directory and are not reachable through the standard postmortem artifact reference chain, leaving the ultimatum evidence effectively invisible to downstream tooling that consumes round-level artifacts.

countBoardReviewIssues (index.ts) and countRoundSeverities (board.ts) use different group-inclusion logic; a future synthetic verdict with an out-of-enum severity would silently diverge review_issue_count across the two postmortem documents.
strengths: All four PM-003 fields (validation_issue_count, self_check_issue_count, review_issue_count, total_issue_count) are now present and correctly populated on all three postmortem writers (writeZeroRoundRunPostmortem, writeRunPostmortem, writeCyclePostmortem) — the multi-round shape divergence from prior rounds is resolved.
component-repair-engine revision mode now fails closed with distinct error messages for both the missing-section and empty-output cases, satisfying the COD-010 boundary contract requirement cleanly.

board.ts writeRunPostmortem now receives validation_issue_count from ctx.validationIssues, eliminating the prior hardcoded-zero regression and making the board-path postmortem consistent with the zero-round path.
writeCyclePostmortem PM-003 total_issue_count now correctly accumulates all three phase counts, so the cycle-level KPI report is no longer undercounting by omitting review-phase findings.
